pod_beacon_alignment_09.title: POD BEACON ALIGNMENT
pod_beacon_alignment_09.description: Match each rescue system with the correct stabilization target.

pod_access_correlation_09.title: POD ACCESS CORRELATION
pod_access_correlation_09.description: Match each pod access record with the correct conclusion.

events.text: Beacon lock unstable. Pod trajectory is drifting.
Or I heard something wearing his voice.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Don't tell me it wasn't him yet.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I know that is irrational. I am choosing to be aware of it and still furious.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Nobody touches that hatch until we know what is moving inside it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.title: PRE-RESCUE BEACON STABILIZATION
events.subtitle: Reducing pod drift before route selection

events.text: That does not make the thing attached to it safer.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: But it means we can hear it better.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: That sounded less comforting out loud.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: We do not have much time. And before anyone asks, no, I do not have a calm version of that sentence.
Said they were just clocks with an ego.
He would absolutely hate this one.

events.title: RESCUE ROUTE MAP
events.content:
ROUTE A
Short Route
High Radiation
Estimated Arrival: Fast
Crew Risk: Severe
Pod Life Support Risk: Moderate

ROUTE B
Long Route
Low Radiation
Estimated Arrival: Delayed
Crew Risk: Moderate
Pod Life Support Risk: Severe
events.caption: Recovered rescue route analysis. Both routes carry significant trade-offs.
events.message: [RESCUE ROUTE MAP ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Route A is fast and radioactive.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Exactly how I wanted to die today.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Route B is slower but less likely to cook you.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: It is deeply annoying that your argument is good.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: But if that life support drops much further, slow becomes a decision too.
events.speaker: MAYA

event.text: Fast route. Terrible route. Very us.
event.speaker: MAYA

event.text: Keep moving. Do not stop in the exposed corridor.
event.speaker: HALE

event.text: You know, Ethan, your concern sounds almost human when it isn't buried under orders.
event.speaker: MAYA

event.text: Not for whoever is in that pod.
event.speaker: MAYA

event.text: Alive people are allowed to keep themselves alive.
event.speaker: HALE

event.text: That sounds like something you say after leaving someone behind.
event.speaker: MAYA

events.title: RESCUE ROUTE TRAVERSAL
events.subtitle: Guiding Maya through selected route toward pod exterior

event.text: Do not let Hale open it first.
event.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Relay to pod. Guidance to trajectory. Life support to biometrics.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Try again before the pod decides to navigate by spite.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.title: POD INTERNAL SCAN REPORT
events.content:
Visual Feed: unavailable
Interior Motion: intermittent
Thermal Bloom: one occupant-sized mass
Suit Seal Trace: present
Occupant Identity: unresolved
Scan Distortion: recursive signal folding detected
events.caption: Interior scan reconstructed without image data. Occupant cannot be visually identified.
events.message: [POD INTERNAL SCAN REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: The scan is trying not to show us a person.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Or trying very hard to invent one.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The scan keeps folding over itself, like the system is trying not to show us a person.
Noah used to say cameras lie less than people.
Cameras lie all the time. They just do it with confidence.
Which is probably a bad sign about my decision-making, but a good sign for whoever is inside.
If this is a trap, I am going to be very annoyed at how emotionally effective it is.
Maya, if you open that pod, you may not be rescuing anyone.
You may be letting something out.
The part where you finally say what you really think.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: For the record, I hate that this is the smart thing to do.
He said waiting was just fear pretending to be organized.
Don't ask me how I forgot saying that until now.
Before Alpha. The pod was empty.
No body. No blood. No suit. Just frost on the inside of the glass.
That is such an Ethan answer.
events.speaker: MAYA

event.text: Then why didn't you tell us?
event.speaker: MAYA

event.text: Because I thought empty meant safe.
event.speaker: HALE

events.title: OLD POD ACCESS LOG
events.content:
Access Event: Emergency Pod Manual Inspection
Timestamp: Before Incident Alpha
Operator: Ethan Hale
Pod State: empty
Interior Residue: frost / no biological matter detected
Seal Reclosure: completed manually
Conflict: current pod telemetry reports active biometric motion
events.caption: Recovered old pod access record. Hale previously opened the same pod before Alpha.
events.message: [OLD POD ACCESS LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Or something was always there and the system learned not to see it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Rescue team has reached emergency pod exterior.

events.title: POD ACCESS RISK REPORT
events.content:
Internal Motion: detected
Seal Pressure: unstable
Manual Override: required
Audio Channel Contamination: rising
External Voice Traffic: unauthorized
Recommendation: correlate access records before opening
events.caption: Recovered pod access risk report.
events.message: [POD ACCESS RISK REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Everything terrible on this station eventually becomes a handle someone has to pull.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Motion means something is inside now.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The old log means I opened it before Alpha.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Seal pressure means manual override. Separate the facts before you open the hatch.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The hatch accepts the override path.
events.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: The pod does not open yet.
events.speaker: SYSTEM

events.title: POD OPENING SEQUENCE
events.subtitle: Releasing seal pressure and stabilizing contaminated audio channel

event.text: Maya, step away from it. Now.
event.speaker: HALE

event.text: Operator, I can't walk away from that.
event.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Relay array stabilizes the pod beacon.
Life support trace follows occupant biometrics.
Do not mix them just because everything is screaming at once.
Old pod access log: I opened it before Alpha.
Seal pressure report: manual override required.
Do not make this simpler than it is.
Simple is how traps get polite.

focus: Episode 9 upgraded to continue Episode 8 V4 intensity
Rescue mission reframed as Noah's possible return
Maya-Noah memories and emotional conflict expanded
Hale becomes more fearful, commanding, and personally conflicted
Route choice made character-driven instead of mechanical
Pod voice/UNKNOWN pressure escalates through the episode
Beacon alignment fail flow made safe with retry and hint node
Pod access fail flow made safe with retry and hint node
Hale's old pod inspection reveal expanded gradually
Open/abort branches made more dramatic and Episode 10-ready
